reasoning: The baseline value for 2025-11-23 is likely ~1.97 (the Friday prior close, as recent momentum pushed spreads up from 1.82 to 1.97). The FRED statistical tool estimates a 33% probability of the series being strictly greater than 1.97 in the near term (2025-11-30), which factors in both recent trend (-4.7e-05 per day) and seasonal patterns (0/1 prior years exceeded). Because the short-term trend and mean reversion (recent avg is ~1.85) both suggest a decline, the probability for the first milestone is low (0.33). As the forecast horizon extends to December 23 and then February 21, the variance of the forecast increases. Furthermore, spreads are historically extremely low at sub-2.00 levels (often averaging 3.0+ over the long term), generating upward risk over longer time horizons. Following the instruction to adjust probabilities closer to 0.50 as uncertainty increases over distant dates, the estimates are stepped up to 0.40 for Dec 2025 and 0.46 for Feb 2026.
